mechanism_narrative: A rising three-month trend in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO momentum falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of up-trend observations.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points indicates a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income and consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings risk rises.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances when UNRATE has risen >0.3pp over 3 months, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate generate a joint monetary policy shock that raises real discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance at the 5% level in a regression predicting 20-day returns, or the combined signal Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a month-over-month decline in retail sales signals that monetary tightening is successfully suppressing real consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week via downward earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Declining RSAFS months within high-inflation regimes do not produce negative 5-day returns in at least 55% of triggered observations, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is positive over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in housing starts reflects strengthening residential investment and construction employment, which boosts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST trends are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations, or the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, restrictive monetary policy sharply raises the equity discount rate and depresses risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS levels above 3.5% are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or the triggered return distribution is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous negative payroll employment growth and a rising unemployment rate produce a recessionary labor market signal that reduces aggregate wage income and corporate profit expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint weakness in PAYEMS and UNRATE is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 45% of instances, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in quarterly real GDP growth indicates broad-based demand strength that lifts corporate earnings across sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 acceleration is followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample observations, or the Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year window.
